title: Humanity after instrumentality evolved or screwed
display_title: Humanity after instrumentality evolved or screwed
wikitext:
[]: Humanity after instrumentality: evolved or screwed?             Quote Post by xdiesp » Thu Aug 26, 2010 8:33 am It strikes me as doubtful, that billions of people turned into liquid would have advanced into evolution. Sharing a single immortal entity has its benefits, that's for sure. But being stuck as liquid? Hello, can't use any tools? What if a thirsty llama comes by? In layman terms: "Aladdin wishes to become immortal, and gets turned into an immortal pool of water".

[]: Re: Humanity after instrumentality: evolved or screwed?             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Aug 26, 2010 9:43 am Depends whether you mean the intended ends of SEELE's system-reset or Gendo's Good End; or the noospheric nirvana of EoTV or the shattered wasteland of EoE (screwed, evolved, evolved, screwed respectively).

[]:             Quote Post by Hawq » Thu Aug 26, 2010 9:51 am Kinda depends on how you view each ending I guess, EoE slightly screwed, can come back but there's a hell of a lot of rebuilding to do & the tang may not be happy it got stopped partway through, EoTV supposedly tangy paradise but everyone can hear your thoughts/feel your feelings etc.. (doesn't sound that great to me). Give me the EoE one any day, come back rebuild & rule the post 3I world.

[]:             Quote Post by Aozora » Thu Aug 26, 2010 11:43 am I was wondering if Seele's idea of human evolution wasn't supposed to end with the Complementation. Something they said made me think they wanted some sort of rebirth afterward. Not sure.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Thu Aug 26, 2010 12:04 pm "End of the evolution is death." SEELE were highly succesful, as a matter of fact.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Thu Aug 26, 2010 12:21 pm Xard wrote: "End of the evolution is death." SEELE were highly succesful, as a matter of fact.

[]: Re: Humanity after instrumentality: evolved or screwed?             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Aug 26, 2010 12:40 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Depends whether you mean the intended ends of SEELE's system-reset or Gendo's Good End; or the noospheric nirvana of EoTV or the shattered wasteland of EoE (screwed, evolved, evolved, screwed respectively).

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Aug 26, 2010 4:44 pm Not only that, but the entity isn't IN the LCL left behind, it's inside the Black Moon. The LCL is the "leftovers" of humanity shedding their physical forms. But yea, it's pretty much death. Instrumentality as generally presented as a single "perfect" consciousness that had no suffering or pleasure, and thus no need or desire to do anything.

[]:             Quote Post by The-Happy-Go-Lucky-Wacko » Thu Aug 26, 2010 6:50 pm But yea, it's pretty much death. Instrumentality as generally presented as a single "perfect" consciousness that had no suffering or pleasure, and thus no need or desire to do anything.

[]: Re: Humanity after instrumentality: evolved or screwed?             Quote Post by Sepulcural Voice » Thu Aug 26, 2010 8:15 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Depends whether you mean the intended ends of SEELE's system-reset or Gendo's Good End; or the noospheric nirvana of EoTV or the shattered wasteland of EoE (screwed, evolved, evolved, screwed respectively).

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Thu Aug 26, 2010 9:09 pm But EoE isn't the extinction of the human race. Who cares if civilization is fucked as long as people are able to understand each other a little better. Yui's views, not mine.

[]:             Quote Post by berto » Fri Aug 27, 2010 12:41 am It also wasn't just humans but all life that was made into one. The plan was to make God and all living things one again. Remember what Shinji said during episode 25 that he felt his couciesness expanding into infinity and how it was a pleasant feeling. Instrumentality is not something that'd be, by nature, bad. It was an attempt to fill the gaps that devide humans, to make all life one so that there'd be no more loneliness, no more pain, and no more death. However, all independence, all distinction amoung the self and others, all that makes humans humans, the good and bad wouldn't exist anymore ether. Good or bad, the answer will vary from person to person depending on their way of looking at life, what they think would be the better way of life. What I want to know is what would happen to that giant manifestation of Lilith that looms above planet Earth if Shinji hadn't regected this little 'forced evolutionary step.' Will she stay there for all time till the ond of all things? Would she fade away? Would she eventually die and decompose while the black moon hovers on the Earth's orbit?

[]:             Quote Post by scarmullet » Fri Aug 27, 2010 12:47 am If you think about it, Only the body was tanged, the mind and the soul remained separate so Life on earth had become lifeforms without form, essentially making Humans into beings of energy, skyrocketing Humanity to a state equivalent to making an Amoeba a Human.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Aug 27, 2010 3:28 am slothen wrote: able to understand each other a little better. Yui's views, not mine.

[]:             Quote Post by xdiesp » Fri Aug 27, 2010 4:59 am scarmullet wrote: skyrocketing Humanity to a state equivalent to making an Amoeba a Human.

[]:             Quote Post by berto » Fri Aug 27, 2010 2:07 pm Leave Earth of their own power? I don't think so, I think that this is what all of those phantom aparitions of Rei were for.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Aug 27, 2010 2:42 pm It is generally implied that the souls of human beings are floating in the LCL-filled sea, and can't do anything except sleep/dream and decide to re-embody. NGE Metaphysics doesn't seem to allow for just free-flying souls to whizz around doing whatever. Also, apparently all non-human life had already materialized before Shinji even rejected Intrumentality, implying that non-human life had been dematerialized by the Anti-AT Field, then immediately rebodied because...well, plants and animals are doing just fine and don't have existential crises.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Aug 27, 2010 3:19 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Also, apparently all non-human life had already materialized before Shinji even rejected Intrumentality, implying

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Aug 27, 2010 4:13 pm My two cents in this thread are with Aura and Slothen, so I don't feel the need to repeat what they said. ^^;

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Aug 28, 2010 1:23 am AuraTwilight wrote: It is generally implied that the souls of human beings are floating in the LCL-filled sea, and can't do anything except sleep/dream and decide to re-embody. NGE Metaphysics doesn't seem to allow for just free-flying souls to whizz around doing whatever.
